description_long: This object is a vintage, mid-century television set with a wooden cabinet exterior and four slender, angled legs. The front features a prominent glass screen displaying a colorful animated scene, surrounded by a thick, dark wood bezel and a vertical panel of mechanical dials and buttons on the right. Two thin metal antennae extend from a black base on the top surface, characteristic of older analog televisions. The sides and back are constructed from a combination of wood and metal, with the rear panel featuring ventilation grilles, a large circular port, and a power cord. The overall design is boxy and robust, with a retro aesthetic typical of televisions from the 1960s or 1970s. The object appears sturdy and functional, suitable for placement in a living room, garage, or as a decorative piece in an urban environment.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a glass screen, metal antennae, and mechanical controls, standing on four angled legs. The design is boxy and retro, with a mix of wood and metal surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass screen with a colorful animated image, thick wood bezel, and a vertical panel of dials and buttons on the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wood grain finish, the protruding back, and the metal antennae on top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the metal ventilation grilles, a large circular port, power cord, and the back panel's utilitarian design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the boxy profile, wood finish, and the angled legs, with less detail than the front.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark wood bezel with vertical mechanical control panel on the right; retro styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Vertical row of dials and buttons on the front right edge.
